_note: IELTS listening test 02 built from public-domain VOA Learning English episodes. Audio streamed from VOA's CDN; questions are original IP. Run: php artisan ingest:voa-listening database/seeders/data/voa_test_02.json --force
title: VOA — Idioms, History, Education, Moon Landing
content: Section 1: idioms from American football · Section 2: Gettysburg battlefield · Section 3: microcredentials · Section 4: private moon lander
attribution: Audio courtesy of VOA Learning English (learningenglish.voanews.com) — public domain. Questions © IELTS Band AI.
license: public domain (US federal government work, commercial reuse permitted with attribution)

questions.question: The idioms in this episode all come from the sport of American ________.
The expression 'to ________ off' means to start an event or project.
In American football, a successful field goal is worth ________ points.
What does the idiom 'to punt' mean according to the host?
options: A. To delay a decision or pass a task to someone else
B. To celebrate a victory loudly
C. To make a final and unchangeable choice
D. To gather a team together
questions.answer: A. To delay a decision or pass a task to someone else

questions.question: The host says a synonym for 'to punt' is 'to ________ it'.
What does 'to move the goalposts' mean?
options: A. Changing the rules so a goal becomes harder to reach
B. Repairing equipment on the field
C. Asking for more time to finish a task
D. Setting up a new sport in a different place
questions.answer: A. Changing the rules so a goal becomes harder to reach

questions.question: A goal that keeps changing is often called a 'moving ________'.
According to the host, what is the key difference between 'moving the goalposts' and 'moving the target'?
options: A. 'Moving the goalposts' is a football-only image, while 'moving the target' is used across many sports
B. 'Moving the goalposts' is older, 'moving the target' is newer
C. 'Moving the goalposts' is positive, 'moving the target' is negative
D. They are exact synonyms in every situation
questions.answer: A. 'Moving the goalposts' is a football-only image, while 'moving the target' is used across many sports

questions.question: An ________ is an expression whose meaning is not the literal sum of its words.
What do most people outside the United States call the game Americans call 'soccer'?
Gettysburg National Military Park is in the eastern part of the state of ________.
The Battle of Gettysburg was fought in the year ________.
How many soldiers in total are said to have fought in the battle?
More than ________ soldiers were dead, wounded or missing after the battle.
Who commanded the Union Army of the Potomac at Gettysburg?
Who commanded the Confederate Army of Northern Virginia?
The Gettysburg Museum holds more than ________ million Civil War artifacts.
What does the famous Gettysburg cyclorama painting depict?
options: A. The death of General Lee
B. The signing of the surrender
D. Lincoln giving his Gettysburg Address

questions.question: President Abraham Lincoln visited the battlefield in November ________ to dedicate a Union burial ground.
How long was Lincoln's famous Gettysburg Address?
The feature explains short professional-certificate programmes called ________.
The international organisation that provides the official definition of a microcredential is ________.
According to the speaker, how long does a typical microcredential take to complete?
options: A. From one day to one week
B. From one week to one month
C. From three months to six months
D. From one to two years
questions.answer: B. From one week to one month

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT named as one of the three most popular microcredential fields?
Which company is NOT mentioned as a major provider of microcredentials?
A Coursera report found that ________ percent of U.S. colleges offering microcredentials also give academic credit.
Which university is given as an example of including certifications in its business programme?
The Coursera monthly fee mentioned in the feature is ________ dollars.
What is the Coursera annual subscription fee mentioned in the feature?
What is the main benefit of microcredentials, according to the speakers?
options: A. They are free for everyone
B. They allow workers to gain narrow, job-ready skills in a short time
C. They replace traditional university degrees
D. They guarantee a salary increase
questions.answer: B. They allow workers to gain narrow, job-ready skills in a short time

questions.question: The private lander discussed in the feature is called Blue ________.
Blue Ghost was built by the Texas-based company Firefly ________.
The lander touched down on the moon on 2 March ________.
The landing site on the near side of the moon is called Mare ________.
How many countries had successfully landed a spacecraft on the moon before this private mission?
Which group lists the five countries that have successfully landed on the moon?
options: A. Russia, U.S., China, India, Japan
B. U.S., U.K., France, Germany, Japan
C. Russia, U.S., China, Israel, India
D. U.S., China, India, Japan, South Korea
questions.answer: A. Russia, U.S., China, India, Japan

questions.question: The lander carried ________ NASA experiments to the lunar surface.
NASA paid about ________ million dollars for the spacecraft itself.
What is the name of the NASA programme under which the mission was paid for?
About how long is one lunar day, which determines the lander's working life on the moon?
